name: D+Q improved old-mouse physical function and post-treatment survival
summary: D+Q has a direct animal healthspan and lifespan anchor: intermittent treatment reduced naturally occurring senescent cells, improved physical function, and increased post-treatment survival in old mice.
statement: In old mice, intermittent oral dasatinib plus quercetin decreased senescent-cell burden and frailty-related proinflammatory cytokine secretion, alleviated physical dysfunction, increased post-treatment survival by 36%, and reduced mortality hazard to 65%.
population_or_model: Naturally aged mice and senescent-cell transplantation mouse models
time_horizon: Intermittent post-treatment survival and physical-function follow-up in old mice
quantitative_note: The abstract reports 36% increased post-treatment survival and mortality hazard reduced to 65% in old mice.
caveats: Animal healthspan and lifespan effects do not establish human longevity benefit.
D+Q effects may depend on tissue, senescent-cell subtype, and disease context.
Financial conflicts and senolytic patents were disclosed.
